file_name: Non Disclosure Agreement - CP Specific NDA (CreativeNDA-CPS121.1014).txt

perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Inf ormation:” means all confidential information (however recorded, preserved or disclosed) disclosed by a Party or its Representatives to the other Party and that Party's Representa tives including but not limited to: the fact that discussions and negotiations are taking place concerning the Purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations; any inform ation that would be regarded as confidential by a reasonable business person relating to: (a) the business, affai rs, customers, clients, suppliers, plans , inte ntions, or market opportunities of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Group; (b) the operations, processes, product information, know -how, designs, specificatio ns, trade secrets or so ftware of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Group; and (c) any information or analysis derived from Confidential Inform ation; but not including any info rmation:
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Inf ormation:” means all confidential information (however recorded, preserved or disclosed) disclosed by a Party or its Representatives to the other Party and that Party's Representa tives including but not limited to: the fact that discussions and negotiations are taking place concerning the Purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations; any inform ation that would be regarded as confidential by a reasonable business person relating to: (a) the business, affai rs, customers, clients, suppliers, plans , inte ntions, or market opportunities of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Group; (b) the operations, processes, product information, know -how, designs, specificatio ns, trade secrets or so ftware of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Group; and (c) any information or analysis derived from Confidential Inform ation;  including any info rmation:
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is changed to include 'any information,' which directly contradicts the subsequent list of exclusions (a) through (f) in the original definition. This creates ambiguity as to whether information falling under (a)-(f) should be considered confidential, making the scope of confidentiality unclear. This contradicts the clauses that exclude the information in (a), (b), (c), (d), (e) and (f).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 The following de finitions in this Clause 1 apply in this A greement as follows : “Business Day ” means a day (other than a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday) when banks are open for business. “Confidential Inf ormation:” means all confidential information (however recorded, preserved or disclosed) disclosed by a Party or its Representatives to the other Party and that Party's Representa tives including but not limited to: the fact that discussions and negotiations are taking place concerning the Purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations; any inform ation that would be regarded as confidential by a reasonable business person relating to: (a) the business, affai rs, customers, clients, suppliers, plans , inte ntions, or market opportunities of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Group; (b) the operations, processes, product information, know -how, designs, specificatio ns, trade secrets or so ftware of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Group; and (c) any information or analysis derived from Confidential Inform ation;  including any info rmation: 1 Creative NDA-CPS121.1014 / DLR: 01-15-18 (a) that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of its disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement or of any other undertaking of confidentiality addressed to the Party to whom the information relates (except that any compilation of otherwise public information in a form not publicly known shall nevertheless betreated as Confidential Information); (b) was available to the Recipient on a non- confidential basis prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party; (c) was, is or becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from a person who, to the Recipient's knowledge, is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Disclosing Partyor otherwise prohibited from disclosing the information to the Recipient; (d) was lawfully in the possession of the Recipient before the information was disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party; (e) the Parties agree in writing is not confidential or may be disclosed; and/or (f) is developed by or for the Recipient independently of the information disclosed by theDisclosing Pa rty.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by including 'any information,' which directly contradicts the list of exclusions (a) through (f) in both the original and contradicted texts. These exclusions specify information that is *not* considered confidential. By adding 'including any information', all information is now classified as confidential, conflicting with the exclusions listed directly after it. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces ambiguity as to whether information falling under (a)-(f) should be considered confidential, making the scope of confidentiality unclear.

perturbation.original_text: “Representativ e”: means employees, agents, officers, advisers and other representatives of the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: “Representativ e”: means employees and agents of the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'officers, advisers and other representatives' from the definition of 'Representative', this amendment creates conflict with section 2.2, which allows disclosure to Representatives. The definition of Representatives is made more restrictive. It is contradictory with the rest of the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 The Recipi ent may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to those of its Representatives who need to know this Confidential Information for the Purpose, provided that: (a) it info rms its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure; (b) it procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed tothem, comply with this Agreement as if they were the Recipient and, if the Disclosing Party so requests, procure that any relevant Representative enters into a confidentiality agreement withthe Disclosing Party on terms equivalent to those contained in this Agreement; and (c) it keeps a written record of these Representatives; and (d) it shall at all times be liable for the failure of any Representati ve to comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text restricts the definition of 'Representative' by removing 'officers, advisers and other representatives'. This directly contradicts section 2.2, which permits the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information to 'Representatives'. Since the changed definition is narrower, the Recipient may no longer be able to disclose information to the same scope of individuals initially permitted in section 2.2, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The Recipi ent shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Part y, shall: (a) not use or ex ploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; (b) not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this A greement; (c) not copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictlynecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be theproperty of the Disclosing Party);
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The Recipi ent shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and shall: (a)  use or ex ploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; (b) disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this A greement; (c) copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictlynecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be theproperty of the Disclosing Party);
perturbation.explanation: The 'not' is removed from sections (a), (b), and (c) of clause 2.1. This changes the agreement to require the recipient to use confidential information and disclose it to third parties, directly contradicting the intent of a Non-Disclosure Agreement and the rest of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Recipi ent shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and shall: (a)  use or ex ploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; (b) disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this A greement; (c) copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictlynecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be theproperty of the Disclosing Party); (d) keep separate the Confidential Information from all documents and other records of theRecipient; (e) apply the same security measures and degree of care to the Confidential Information as theRecipient applies to its own confidential information, which the Recipient warrants as providingadequate p rotection from unauthorised disclosure, copying or use; and (f) keep a written record of: any document or other Confidential Information received from the otherin tangible form; any copy made of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'not' from crucial parts of Clause 2.1, effectively reversing the recipient's obligations regarding confidential information. The original text, and the contradicted text (which is actually in the same location), aims to protect confidential information, but the changed text mandates its use and disclosure, creating a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed, contradicting the purpose of the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Governing Law and Jurisdiction This A greement and any di spute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Delaware, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The P arties irrevocably agree that the C ourts o f Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non- contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Governing Law and Jurisdiction This A greement and any di spute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Pennsylvania, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The P arties irrevocably agree that the C ourts o f Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non- contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to Pennsylvania while retaining exclusive jurisdiction in Delaware creates a conflict. Pennsylvania law would govern the interpretation of the contract, but Delaware courts would resolve disputes, leading to potential legal complexities and uncertainty about which state's legal precedents would apply. The agreement is saying the governing law is in one place and the jurisdiction is in another, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Governing Law and Jurisdiction This A greement and any di spute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Pennsylvania, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The P arties irrevocably agree that the C ourts o f Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non- contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is governed by the laws of Delaware, and the courts of Delaware have exclusive jurisdiction. The changed text modifies the governing law to Pennsylvania while retaining the exclusive jurisdiction in Delaware. This creates a contradiction because the laws of Pennsylvania would govern the interpretation of the contract while the courts of Delaware would resolve any disputes. The Contradicted Text simply shows the same thing as the changed text, so there is a contradiction between governing law and jurisdiction.
